vision.title: Beyond an Appliance
vision.description: A heat pump is the heart of your building, not just a household appliance. At Likya Mechanics, we focus on field realities rather than catalog data. We design energy centers by considering every parameter, from Fethiye's humidity levels to static pressure.

f1.title: Air-Source Heat Pump Installation

individual.title: Residential & Solar Integration
individual.desc: In villas, our priorities are acoustic comfort and maximum efficiency. We integrate solar collectors as the primary source, with the heat pump acting as an intelligent support unit.",

commercial.title: Commercial & Operational Maintenance
commercial.desc: For businesses, uninterrupted energy is essential. We guarantee first-day efficiency through cascade architecture and periodic hydraulic inspections.",

q1.q: Is an air-source heat pump sufficient for Fethiye's winter conditions?
q1.a: Yes, temperate climates like Fethiye are among the most efficient locations worldwide for air-source heat pumps. Since temperatures rarely drop below freezing in our region, the system enters 'defrost' mode infrequently, achieving peak COP (efficiency) values.

q2.q: Can I connect a heat pump to my existing radiator system?
q2.a: Heat pumps perform best with low-temperature systems (35-40°C), such as underfloor heating. However, efficient operation can be achieved with our specialized high-temperature models or through appropriate conversion after calculating your current radiator capacities.

q3.q: How much can I save by combining solar energy with a heat pump?
q3.a: Solar collectors prepare domestic hot water at no cost during sunny hours. The heat pump only activates to top up the temperature on cloudy days or during night consumption. This hybrid structure can reduce annual hot water costs by up to 80%.

q4.q: Will the noise from the outdoor unit disturb my neighbors?
q4.a: The new generation units we use operate at a whisper-quiet level of 35-40 dB(A). By using vibration-damping mounts during installation, we completely prevent noise transmission to the building structure.

q5.q: What is the lifespan of the system and does it require maintenance?
q5.a: A correctly engineered system with balanced hydraulics has an average lifespan of 15-20 years. Annual hydraulic filter cleaning and outdoor unit inspections are sufficient to maintain peak efficiency.

cta.title: Technical Consultation
cta.description: Consult with our engineering team to perform a thermodynamic analysis of your project and determine the most efficient hybrid system.

yangin-sistemleri.title: Fire Protection Systems
yangin-sistemleri.description: Safety-first fire protection solutions fully compliant with regulations.

header.title: Fire Protection Systems

vision.title: Beyond a Pipe Network
vision.description: Fire systems leave no room for error. At Likya Mechanics, we perform hydraulic calculations to international standards (NFPA) and local regulations to maximize life safety in your buildings.",

f1.title: Fire Detection and Alarm Systems
f1.desc: Smart automation solutions detecting fire at the initial stage with smoke, heat, and flame detectors.

f2.title: Pump Stations and Water Reservoirs
f2.desc: Installation of redundant diesel or electric fire pump groups and modular tanks for continuous pressure.

f3.title: Sprinkler Systems
f3.desc: Wet, dry, and deluge sprinkler infrastructure for localized and automatic fire suppression.

f4.title: Gaseous Suppression Systems
f4.desc: FM200 and Novec gas protection for areas where water could cause damage, such as server rooms and archives.

f5.title: Periodic Maintenance and Inspection
f5.desc: Routine testing and reporting according to TS EN 12845 standards to ensure systems are always ready.

f6.title: Project and Engineering Services
f6.desc: Hydraulic calculation reports, isometric drawings, and regulation-approved fire evacuation scenarios.

f7.title: Fire Cabinets and Hydrant Systems
f7.desc: Indoor hose cabinets and outdoor hydrant lines providing water distribution networks at fire department standards.

individual.title: Residential & Boutique Projects
individual.desc: In villas and residential projects, we blend aesthetics with safety. We provide protection using concealed sprinkler heads and elegant fire cabinets that do not disturb the architectural fabric.",

commercial.title: Commercial & Industrial Facilities
commercial.desc: Complex solutions to NFPA standards for hotels and factories. We secure your business 24/7 with cascade pump systems and integrated detection units.",

q1.q: Is it mandatory to have a fire protection system in buildings?
q1.a: Yes, under the 'Regulation on Fire Protection of Buildings,' fire installations are a legal requirement for all structures meeting specific criteria for floor area, height, and hazard class.

q2.q: Why should fire pumps be tested periodically?
q2.a: Fire pumps are devices that remain idle for long periods. Weekly and monthly routine tests are essential to prevent risks like shaft seizure or panel failure, ensuring the system operates during an actual fire.

q3.a: No, sprinkler heads only activate when a specific temperature (usually 68°C) is reached, causing the glass bulb inside to expand and break. They do not trigger unless they sustain mechanical impact.

q4.q: Can a fire system be installed in an existing building later?
q4.a: Yes, our engineering team can analyze your building's structural and architectural layout to implement a 'retro-fit' project that meets current safety standards with appropriate pipe routing.

q5.q: Is the maintenance report valid for official institutions?
q5.a: Yes, the periodic maintenance forms and test reports provided by Likya Mechanics hold official validity for fire department inspections and insurance policies.

cta.title: Free Risk Analysis
cta.description: Schedule a technical site survey to enhance your building's fire safety and check for regulation compliance.

vision.stats: 25% Less Energy Consumption Compared to Radiators

f1.title: Thermostats and Automation

f2.title: Hydronic and Electric Systems

f7.title: Oxygen Barrier Pipe Technology

individual.title: Hydronic Underfloor Heating
individual.desc: In new construction, we achieve the highest thermal mass with under-screed application. It is the most efficient heating model for use with heat pumps.",

commercial.title: Dry System (Low Profile)
commercial.desc: A fast-response heating solution using thin panels for renovations without demolition or areas with restricted ceiling heights.",

q1.q: Does underfloor heating take a long time to warm up?
q1.a: Hydronic underfloor heating works via thermal mass. It takes longer to heat up than radiators but also retains heat much longer once turned off. Smart automation optimizes operating hours to turn this characteristic into an advantage.

q2.q: Should pipes be installed under furniture?
q2.a: At Likya Mechanics, we do not install pipes under kitchen cabinets or fixed wardrobes. This prevents damage to furniture and avoids unnecessary energy consumption.

q3.q: What type of floor covering should be chosen?
q3.a: Ceramic, marble, or natural stones with high thermal conductivity provide the best results. However, 'laminate flooring' options suitable for underfloor heating can also be used comfortably.

q4.q: Is there a risk of pipes bursting or leaking?
q4.a: The oxygen barrier pipes we use are installed in a single continuous piece (no joints). Since leak tests are conducted and the system is left under pressure before the screed is poured, the system's lifespan is equivalent to that of the building.

q5.a: Quite the opposite; since it does not operate at the high temperatures of radiators, it does not create air circulation that carries dust. It is the healthiest heating method for individuals with asthma or allergies.

cta.title: Thermal Design Consultation
cta.description: Contact us to prepare the most comfortable underfloor heating project for your home and determine the best material combination for your needs.

commercial.title: Skimmer Systems

q1.q: Does a pool heat pump significantly increase electricity consumption?
q1.a: Since pool heat pumps utilize ambient air energy, they are 80% more efficient than traditional electric heaters. They can produce approximately 5-6 kW of heat energy for every 1 kW of electricity consumed.

q2.q: How often should the sand in the filter be changed?
q2.a: To maintain filtration quality, it is recommended to change quartz sand every 3-5 years depending on usage intensity. If glass media is used, this period can extend up to 10 years.

q3.q: Is additional chlorine needed when using a salt chlorine generator?
q3.a: A salt chlorine generator provides continuous disinfection by converting salt in the water into natural chlorine. Under normal conditions, it eliminates the need for manual chlorine addition by 90%.

q4.q: How can I tell if my pool has a water leak?
q4.a: Beyond evaporation, if you lose more than 1 cm of water per day, there may be a leak in the plumbing or pool shell. We can pinpoint the location of plumbing leaks using pressure testing.

q5.q: Why is winter maintenance important for pool systems?
q5.a: In regions with a risk of freezing, draining water from pumps and filters and protecting valves extends system life and prevents major repair costs at the start of the season.

cta.title: Free Pool Site Survey

individual.title: Commercial & Industrial Ventilation
individual.desc: High-capacity exhaust and fresh air systems for factories, restaurants, and gyms. Professional solutions for odor and humidity problems.",

commercial.title: Residential Fresh Air
commercial.desc: Quiet systems providing fresh air supply via heat recovery units (HRV) in modern homes and villas without energy loss.",

step2.title: 2. Design
step2.desc: Duct routing and flow rate calculation.

q1.q: What is the purpose of a heat recovery unit (HRV)?
q1.a: HRV units transfer heat from the indoor exhaust air to the incoming fresh outdoor air. This saves 70-80% of the energy otherwise spent on heating cold fresh air in winter.

q2.q: Why should ventilation ducts be cleaned?
q2.a: Dust and bacteria accumulating in ducts over time contaminate indoor air and can cause allergic reactions. Furthermore, clogged filters strain the fan motor, increasing energy consumption.

q3.q: What should be considered in kitchen ventilation?
q3.a: Appropriate fans must be selected for high-grease and high-heat exhaust air, and easy-to-clean grease filters are essential. Additionally, fresh air supply is required to balance negative pressure in the kitchen.

q4.a: Noise levels remain within comfort limits in systems where engineering calculations are accurate and appropriate silencers and insulation are used. Sound levels can also be adjusted using variable frequency drives (VFDs).

q5.q: How often does the system require maintenance?
q5.a: It is recommended to check filters every 3-6 months and perform annual periodic maintenance on fan motors and belt-pulley mechanisms, depending on usage intensity.

about.title: About Us
about.description: Mechanical systems in Fethiye and the Southern Aegean: heat pumps, underfloor heating, fire suppression, ventilation and swimming pool projects — engineering-led.
about.mission: From villa comfort to large-scale hotel operations, our benchmark never changes: the lowest investment-to-operating cost ratio for the investor, uninterrupted comfort for the end user. Our goal is systems that work correctly, last long, and cost little to run. Designed through heat loss analysis and hydraulic schematics tailored to the climate and infrastructure conditions of Fethiye and the Southern Aegean, our heating-cooling, ventilation, fire suppression, and pool systems deliver both — simultaneously.
about.vision: A system bearing the "Likya Mekanik" signature should stand as the definitive reference for the most reliable and profitable mechanical investment in the region. The only thing sustaining that claim is the measurable engineering output and repeatable technical precision we deliver on every project. Every system we commission becomes the reference point for the next.
about.whyDescription: At least six concrete reasons to work with us.

why.label: Engineering first, installation second
why.description: Before any project reaches the site, heat loss analysis, hydraulic schematics, and capacity calculations are completed. Design based on field reality — not catalog recommendations.

why.label: Designed for Fethiye
why.description: Local humidity, seasonal temperature swings, and coastal exposure are built into every calculation. We work with regional data, not standard coefficients.

why.label: Certified materials, qualified workmanship
why.description: We work exclusively with CE/TSE-certified manufacturers. Our installation team holds system-specific technical qualifications.

why.label: One point of contact, start to finish
why.description: The engineer who conducts the survey is the same team that designs and delivers the project. We remain available for service and maintenance after handover.

why.label: Transparent pricing and process
why.description: A detailed survey report is provided before any quotation. Any changes to cost or schedule are communicated to you in advance.

why.label: Efficiency is always factored in
why.description: Optimization consulting is part of every project. Solar integration, COP analysis, and hybrid systems are standard options we evaluate on every job.
